item.title: auto and run commands support --category filter to scope item selection
item.intent: Users running auto or run on a mixed backlog can target a single category (e.g., auto done --category documentation) so a sprint session stays focused. Only items matching the given category are eligible — unmatched items are skipped as if they don't exist in that run.
item.description: Users with large, categorized backlogs have no way to run a focused sprint on one area. Without a category filter, auto done picks any ready item regardless of category — mixing documentation bugs with feature work in the same session. A --category flag scopes item selection to only items tagged with that category, enabling focused sprint sessions by area (documentation, performance, testing, etc.).
steps: Add --category <name> flag to cmd_run and cmd_auto option parsing
When --category is set, filter eligible items in pick_next_item to only those where item.category == category_filter
Pass category filter through the auto loop so each iteration respects the scope
If no items match the category filter, print a clear message and exit (not a circuit-breaker failure)
Update help text for run and auto commands to document --category
acceptanceCriteria: auto done --category documentation runs only documentation-tagged items; other items are not touched
auto done --category nonexistent exits with a clear 'no items found' message, not an error
run --category <name> with a specific ID rejects the item if it does not match the category
help text for run and auto shows the --category flag
